text:
Imagine a peculiar town known as Glitterburg. Children would dance around in vibrant carnival masks, and adults wore colorful hats. Every year, on the Feast of Blossoms, each resident got a balloon decoration showcasing their favorite trait. The highlight was the Float Festival, where everyone would gather on the town square to float down a rainbow-laden stream in specially decorated boats built by their hands.

One year, a girl named Lily won the prized Gold Glitter Globe. It shimmered with flecks of gold and had a tiny pearl at its center. Lily was determined to keep it forever in a pocket-sized champagne flute. Her family noticed that she seemed overly fascinated with the little pearl, which they believed symbolized her dreams.

On the Eve of Blossoms, Lily set off to the shore before dawn to impress her grandpa with her unique gift. It was cold, and her balloon flew away. Panicked, she rushed to look for it. It drifted further away before she realized maybe she shouldn
